about.subtitle2: Since 2001, we have helped companies across many sectors improve their infrastructure, protect their systems and move forward with their digital transformation — with a close, professional and personalised approach.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: We answer the most common questions. If you can't find what you're looking for, write to us.

items.q: Do you only work in Madrid or across Spain?
items.a: Our headquarters are in Madrid, where we offer both on-site and remote service. For the rest of Spain, we work remotely with the same quality and response time.

items.q: Do you offer both on-site and remote support?
items.a: Yes. We adapt the service mode to each client: remote access for common incidents and on-site when the situation requires it.

items.q: Do you work with small SMEs?
items.a: Yes. SMEs are our main clients. No company is too small if it has real technology needs.

items.q: Can you act as an external IT department?
items.a: Yes. Many of our clients don't have an internal IT department. We take on that role, either fully or partially, based on each organization's needs.
Yes. We analyze the company's security posture, identify vulnerabilities and propose corrective and preventive measures tailored to each case.

items.q: Do you install routers, WiFi repeaters and business networks?
items.a: Yes. We design, install and maintain professional network infrastructures, including WiFi, cabling, network electronics and telecommunications.

items.q: Do you offer ongoing IT maintenance?
items.a: Yes. We offer preventive and corrective maintenance, monitoring and incident management to keep your business running.
Yes. We develop software, applications and digital solutions adapted to the real processes of each company, including APIs and integrations.

items.q: Can you improve an old website?
items.a: Yes. We redesign, optimize or rebuild corporate websites focused on performance, security, SEO and user experience.

items.q: Do you work with hotels, restaurants, schools and law firms?
items.a: Yes. We have experience in multiple sectors: hospitality, education, professional firms, agencies, retail and service companies.

items.q: What sets Onilusion apart from other IT companies?
items.a: Real personalization. We don't sell closed packages. We listen, analyze and design specific solutions for each client. We've been doing it for over two decades.

items.q: How do I request a quote?
items.a: You can contact us via form, email or phone. Tell us your situation, we'll analyze it and present a proposal tailored to your needs and budget.

legal.title: Legal Notice
legal.name: Onilusion S.A.
legal.activity: Provision of consulting, advisory and advertising services in informatics, electronics and telecommunications, as well as research and development of computer applications and systems.

sections.p: Accessing and using this website grants the status of user and implies acceptance of these terms. Users agree to make appropriate use of the content and not to use it for unlawful activities or against good faith.

The contents of this website (texts, images, logos, design and code) are owned by Onilusion S.A. or by third parties who have authorised their use, and are protected by intellectual and industrial property law. Reproduction without express authorisation is prohibited.

Onilusion S.A. is not liable for damages arising from improper use of this website or for the content of third-party sites linked from it. We work to keep the information up to date and accurate, without guaranteeing the complete absence of errors.
These terms are governed by Spanish law. Any dispute shall be submitted to the courts of Madrid, unless applicable regulations provide otherwise.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: Information about the processing of your personal data

sections.p: Onilusion S.A., VAT ID A82874603, registered at Calle Doctor Esquerdo, 105, 28007 Madrid (Spain). Email: info@onilusion.com. Phone: +34 914 009 634.
Data provided through the contact form is processed to handle your enquiry or quote request and to manage any resulting business relationship. No automated decision-making or profiling takes place.
The legal basis for processing is the data subject's consent (art. 6.1.a GDPR), given by ticking the acceptance box on the form, and the legitimate interest in responding to enquiries received.
Data is kept for as long as necessary to handle your request and, thereafter, for the legally required periods.
Data is not shared with third parties except where legally required. No international data transfers take place.

You may exercise your rights of access, rectification, erasure, objection, restriction of processing and portability by writing to info@onilusion.com, providing proof of identity. You may also lodge a complaint with the Spanish Data Protection Agency (www.aepd.es).

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.subtitle: Information about the cookies we use

sections.p: Cookies are small files stored on your device when you visit a website, allowing it to remember information about your visit, such as language or preferences.

This site only uses cookies and technical storage necessary for its operation: your language preference and your decision regarding cookie use. We do not use advertising cookies. If analytics cookies are added in the future, they will only be activated with your prior consent.
You can accept or reject non-essential cookies from the settings banner. You can also delete or block cookies from your browser settings at any time.
This policy may be updated to reflect regulatory or technical changes. We recommend reviewing it periodically.
